skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
stem: The triangle inequality theorem states that the sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the length of the third side. If a triangle has side lengths of 8 and 15, which inequality represents the possible lengths, x, of the third side of the triangle?
rationale: Choice A is correct. By the triangle inequality, 8 + x > 15 gives x > 7; 8 + 15 > x gives x < 23; and 15 + x > 8 gives x > -7 (automatically satisfied). Combining yields 7 < x < 23. Choice B omits the lower bound. Choice C ignores the lower bound and reverses the upper bound. Choice D includes values outside the valid range.
